summary: The suite combines well-focused unit tests (classify_secret, parse_config, build_report, renderers, CLI) with a thorough act-based end-to-end harness that asserts exact JSON/Markdown/notify output values per fixture case. Boundary conditions on expiry classification (exactly-on-expiry, day-after) and sorted ordering are tested. The _secret() helper and Case dataclass keep fixtures DRY and readable, and structural/actionlint checks are a nice addition. Gaps: --strict exit-code behavior (a documented feature) is untested in both layers; --output file-write path is untested; the CLI negative --warning-days guard has no test; parse_config edge cases for non-dict root, boolean policy_days, and non-string required_by entries exist in the implementation but have no corresponding tests; an empty secrets list is never exercised; and the Markdown table structure (column headers, row ordering in warning/ok) is verified only by string presence rather than position. The unit test import assumes PYTHONPATH includes the project root, which could silently break in fresh CI environments. Overall a solid, trustworthy suite that would catch the vast majority of regressions, let down mainly by incomplete error-path and CLI-flag coverage.
